***Percy Jackson & the Olympians: The Lightning Thief*** (also known as ***Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief***) is a 2010 [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_film "Fantasy film") [adventure film](/wiki/Adventure_film "Adventure film") directed by [Chris Columbus](/wiki/Chris_Columbus_(filmmaker) "Chris Columbus (filmmaker)") and written by [Craig Titley](/wiki/Craig_Titley "Craig Titley"), based on [the 2005 novel](/wiki/The_Lightning_Thief "The Lightning Thief") by [Rick Riordan](/wiki/Rick_Riordan "Rick Riordan"). It is the first installment in the [*Percy Jackson* film series](/wiki/Percy_Jackson_(film_series) "Percy Jackson (film series)"), and stars [Logan Lerman](/wiki/Logan_Lerman "Logan Lerman") as [Percy Jackson](/wiki/Percy_Jackson "Percy Jackson"), alongside [Brandon T. Jackson](/wiki/Brandon_T._Jackson "Brandon T. Jackson"), [Alexandra Daddario](/wiki/Alexandra_Daddario "Alexandra Daddario"), [Jake Abel](/wiki/Jake_Abel "Jake Abel"), [Sean Bean](/wiki/Sean_Bean "Sean Bean"), [Pierce Brosnan](/wiki/Pierce_Brosnan "Pierce Brosnan"), [Steve Coogan](/wiki/Steve_Coogan "Steve Coogan"), [Rosario Dawson](/wiki/Rosario_Dawson "Rosario Dawson"), [Catherine Keener](/wiki/Catherine_Keener "Catherine Keener"), [Kevin McKidd](/wiki/Kevin_McKidd "Kevin McKidd"), [Joe Pantoliano](/wiki/Joe_Pantoliano "Joe Pantoliano"), and [Uma Thurman](/wiki/Uma_Thurman "Uma Thurman").

In 2004, 20th Century Fox acquired the film rights to the book series. In 2007, Chris Columbus was hired to direct. Filming began April 2009 in Vancouver. Some shots were filmed in Tennessee and Las Vegas, and some exterior scenes were shot in New York.

*Percy Jackson & the Olympians: The Lightning Thief* was released theatrically in the United States on February 12, 2010, by [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox").[[5]](#cite_note-5) It received mixed reviews from critics, with praise for the action sequences and the performances of Lerman and Jackson, but criticism for its script and unfaithfulness to the source material. The film grossed $226.4 million worldwide against a production budget of $95 million.[[4]](#cite_note-bom-4) It was released on June 29, 2010, on [DVD](/wiki/DVD-Video "DVD-Video")[[6]](#cite_note-DVD-6) and [Blu-ray Disc](/wiki/Blu-ray_Disc "Blu-ray Disc").[[7]](#cite_note-Bluray-7) A video game based on the film was released for [Nintendo DS](/wiki/Nintendo_DS "Nintendo DS") on February 11, 2010. A sequel, *[Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters](/wiki/Percy_Jackson:_Sea_of_Monsters "Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters")*, was released on August 7, 2013.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

At the top of the [Empire State Building](/wiki/Empire_State_Building "Empire State Building"), [Zeus](/wiki/Zeus "Zeus") meets his brother [Poseidon](/wiki/Poseidon "Poseidon"). Zeus accuses Poseidon's [demigod](/wiki/Demigod "Demigod") son, [Percy Jackson](/wiki/Percy_Jackson "Percy Jackson"), of stealing his master [lightning bolt](/wiki/Thunderbolt "Thunderbolt"). Poseidon reminds him that Percy is unaware of his true identity. However, Zeus declares that unless the bolt is returned to [Mount Olympus](/wiki/Mount_Olympus "Mount Olympus") before midnight of the [Summer Solstice](/wiki/Summer_Solstice "Summer Solstice"), war will be waged between all the gods.

16-year-old Percy struggles with [dyslexia](/wiki/Dyslexia "Dyslexia") and [ADHD](/wiki/Attention_deficit_hyperactivity_disorder "Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder"). On a school trip to the [MOMA](/wiki/Museum_of_Modern_Art "Museum of Modern Art"), he is attacked by [Alecto](/wiki/Alecto "Alecto"), a [Fury](/wiki/Erinyes "Erinyes") masquerading as his substitute English teacher Mrs. Dodds, who demands the bolt. Percy's best friend [Grover Underwood](/wiki/List_of_Camp_Half-Blood_characters#Grover_Underwood "List of Camp Half-Blood characters") and his Latin teacher Mr. Brunner help scare off Alecto. His teacher gives Percy a very powerful cosmic weapon which looks like a pen, and instructs Grover to take him and his mother, Sally to Camp Half-Blood — a secret sanctuary for demigod children on [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), leaving behind Sally's abusive husband, Percy's stepfather, Gabe Ugliano.

On the way there in Sally's car, they are attacked by the [Minotaur](/wiki/Minotaur "Minotaur"), who seemingly kills Percy's mother. Percy discovers the pen is a magical sword, and uses it to fight off the Minotaur, killing it with its own horn and goes into shock. Waking up three days later, Percy learns he is Poseidon's son, Grover is a [satyr](/wiki/Satyr "Satyr") and Percy's protector, and Mr. Brunner is [Chiron](/wiki/Chiron "Chiron"), a [centaur](/wiki/Centaur "Centaur"). He starts coming into his latent demigod powers, which include [hydrokinesis](/wiki/Hydrokinesis "Hydrokinesis") and healing, and meets other demigods, including [Annabeth Chase](/wiki/List_of_Camp_Half-Blood_characters#Annabeth_Chase "List of Camp Half-Blood characters"), [Athena](/wiki/Athena "Athena")'s daughter; and the camp's leader [Luke Castellan](/wiki/List_of_Camp_Half-Blood_characters#Luke_Castellan "List of Camp Half-Blood characters"), [Hermes](/wiki/Hermes "Hermes")'s son.

Percy is visited by his uncle [Hades](/wiki/Hades "Hades")'s apparition, who reveals that the Minotaur abducted Sally to the [Underworld](/wiki/Underworld "Underworld") to trade for the bolt. Defying Chiron's orders, Percy sets out for the Underworld with Grover and Annabeth. Luke gives Percy a map with the location of three green pearls belonging to Hades' wife (and Percy's aunt) [Persephone](/wiki/Persephone "Persephone"), which will allow them to escape the Underworld. He also gives him a pair of flying winged [Converse All-Stars](/wiki/Converse_All-Stars "Converse All-Stars") stolen from Hermes, and Luke's favorite shield. Meanwhile, Percy is declared missing.

At a garden center in [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"), with help from Grover and Annabeth, Percy manages to decapitate [Medusa](/wiki/Medusa "Medusa") and takes the first pearl from her corpse. At the [Parthenon in Nashville](/wiki/Parthenon_(Nashville) "Parthenon (Nashville)"), Percy uses the shoes to retrieve the second pearl from the crown of a statue of Athena there, and Grover kills a [Hydra](/wiki/Lernaean_Hydra "Lernaean Hydra") with Medusa's head. The trio arrives at the Lotus Hotel and Casino in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") to obtain the third pearl; however, they forget their mission after eating [lotus flowers](/wiki/Lotus_flowers "Lotus flowers"), the effects of which cause them to lose their sense of time. Percy snaps out of the spell after hearing the voice of Poseidon through telepathy telling him not to eat any more flowers.

Percy frees Grover and Annabeth from the flowers' effects; they locate the final pearl in the casino and escape. Unfortunately, they only have one day left to prevent the war, as they were in the casino for almost a week. They discover that the Underworld is in [Hollywood](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles") so the trio race there. With all three pearls, Percy, Grover, and Annabeth enter the portal to the Underworld upon arriving in Hollywood. There, Hades finds the bolt hidden inside Luke's shield, revealing that Luke was the thief all along.

Hades tries to kill the trio, but Persephone turns on him in retaliation for imprisoning her and gives the bolt to Percy. As they only have three pearls, Grover remains behind, while Percy, Annabeth, and Sally teleport to the Empire State Building, the secret entrance to Olympus. However, before they can enter, they are ambushed by Luke, who reveals that he stole the bolt to demolish Olympus and establish the demigods (and himself) as Olympus' new rulers. After a battle across [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), Percy defeats Luke, returns the bolt to Zeus, and reconciles with his father. Having been reunited with Grover, Percy and Annabeth continue to train back at Camp Half-Blood.

In a mid-credits scene, Sally has kicked Gabe out of their apartment. When going to the locked refrigerator to get a drink, he ignores Percy's written warning to not open it, breaks it open, and is turned into stone by Medusa's head, which he kept inside.

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/Chris_Columbus_-_Home_Alone_35th.jpg/250px-Chris_Columbus_-_Home_Alone_35th.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chris_Columbus_-_Home_Alone_35th.jpg)

[Chris Columbus](/wiki/Chris_Columbus_(filmmaker) "Chris Columbus (filmmaker)") is the film's director and producer.

In June 2004, [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox") acquired the film rights to the book.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In April 2007, [Chris Columbus](/wiki/Chris_Columbus_(filmmaker) "Chris Columbus (filmmaker)") was hired to direct.[[14]](#cite_note-struck-14) Filming began in April 2009 in [Vancouver](/wiki/Vancouver "Vancouver"),[[11]](#cite_note-bulk-11) and Mammoth Studios in [Burnaby](/wiki/Burnaby,_British_Columbia "Burnaby, British Columbia") was selected to be the studio.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Portions of the film were shot at the [Parthenon](/wiki/Parthenon_(Nashville) "Parthenon (Nashville)") in [Nashville, Tennessee](/wiki/Nashville,_Tennessee "Nashville, Tennessee"), that has a full-scale replica of the [original Parthenon](/wiki/Parthenon "Parthenon") in [Athens](/wiki/Athens "Athens").[[16]](#cite_note-16) The Lotus Casino sequence was filmed at [The Westin Bayshore](/wiki/The_Westin_Bayshore "The Westin Bayshore") in Vancouver in June 2009, and additional scenes of Percy, Grover and Annabeth driving to and from the casino were shot on the [Las Vegas Strip](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Strip "Las Vegas Strip") and in front of the [Fremont Street Experience](/wiki/Fremont_Street_Experience "Fremont Street Experience"). Filming wrapped up on the morning of July 25, 2009, in Mission, British Columbia. Additional exterior scenes were filmed on location in [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York") during the first week of August 2009.[[17]](#cite_note-17) [Digital intermediate](/wiki/Digital_intermediate "Digital intermediate") work began in San Francisco in November 2009.[[18]](#cite_note-18) [Christophe Beck](/wiki/Christophe_Beck "Christophe Beck") composed the score.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Columbus has stated that the cast was chosen specifically with sequels in mind. "I think with Percy Jackson it was a matter of finding the right cast to fit into these roles, sort of the perfect cast for these roles, because hopefully, God willing, we will go on to do other Percy Jackson films and you want the cast to grow with their characters".[[20]](#cite_note-ColumbusExclusive-20)

During production, Riordan was disappointed with the changes made to the story and warned the studio that it would likely alienate the readers of the book series that it was depending on to buy tickets. In two emails commenting at length on a draft of the script that he posted to his blog in 2018, he specifically warned the studio that trying to make the story more attractive to a teenage audience by aging the characters and including some profanity in the script might move a significant portion of the books' readers to leave the theater in disgust long before the movie ended. He also felt the introduction of [Persephone](/wiki/Persephone "Persephone")'s pearls as a plot device made no sense, having no basis in mythology and distracting Percy from his goal of recovering the stolen lightning bolt.[[21]](#cite_note-Riordan_blog_post-21)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Reception")]

### Box office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Box office")]

The film opened on February 12, 2010, in 3,356 theaters; its opening weekend box-office results totaled $31.2 million[[4]](#cite_note-bom-4) in the U.S., finishing at #3 below *[The Wolfman](/wiki/The_Wolfman_(2010_film) "The Wolfman (2010 film)")*, which opened at #2 with $31.5 million and below *[Valentine's Day](/wiki/Valentine%27s_Day_(2010_film) "Valentine's Day (2010 film)")*, which opened at #1 with $56.3 million.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The film had a strong opening weekend for its genre, posting the highest opening weekend for a fantasy film not from the *[Harry Potter](/wiki/Harry_Potter_(film_series) "Harry Potter (film series)")*, *[Chronicles of Narnia](/wiki/The_Chronicles_of_Narnia_(film_series) "The Chronicles of Narnia (film series)")*, or *[Lord of the Rings](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings_(film_series) "The Lord of the Rings (film series)")* series.[[23]](#cite_note-23) As of September 14, 2010, it grossed a total of $88.8 million in the U.S. and Canada with $137.7 million elsewhere in the world, bringing it to $226.5 million.[[4]](#cite_note-bom-4)

### Critical response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Critical response")]

On [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes") the film has an approval rating of 48% based on reviews from 150 critics, with an average score of 5.30/10. The site's consensus reads: "Though it may seem like just another Harry Potter knockoff, *Percy Jackson* benefits from a strong supporting cast, a speedy plot, and plenty of fun with Greek mythology."[[24]](#cite_note-24) On [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic") it has a score of 47 out of 100, based on 31 reviews, indicating "mixed or average reviews".[[25]](#cite_note-25) Audiences polled by [CinemaScore](/wiki/CinemaScore "CinemaScore") gave the film a grade B+ on a scale from A+ to F.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

[Kenneth Turan](/wiki/Kenneth_Turan "Kenneth Turan") of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* described the film as "standard Hollywood product... unadventurous and uninteresting".[[27]](#cite_note-27) The reviewer for *[The Washington Post](/wiki/The_Washington_Post "The Washington Post")* thought "the movie suffers by taking itself a little too seriously. It's not just that it's a lot less funny than the book. It's also a lot less fun".[[28]](#cite_note-28) On BBC Radio 5, [Mark Kermode](/wiki/Mark_Kermode "Mark Kermode") criticized the similarity of the film to director Chris Columbus's *Harry Potter* films, likening it to a *Harry Potter* parody book and dubbing it *Benjamin Sniddlegrass and the Cauldron of Penguins*.[[29]](#cite_note-29) This comment later sparked a satirical [fan creation](/wiki/Benjamin_Sniddlegrass_and_the_Cauldron_of_Penguins "Benjamin Sniddlegrass and the Cauldron of Penguins") with precisely that title, narrated by [Stephen Fry](/wiki/Stephen_Fry "Stephen Fry").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

#### Rick Riordan's response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Rick Riordan's response")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Rick_riordan_2007.jpg/250px-Rick_riordan_2007.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rick_riordan_2007.jpg)

Author Rick Riordan expressed strong dislike for the film, criticizing its deviations and unfaithfulness to the source material.

The author, [Rick Riordan](/wiki/Rick_Riordan "Rick Riordan"), publicly criticized the final script. He revealed email recommendations for script changes with names redacted. Regarding future support for a reboot Riordan said, "In the future, if some project actually does get underway, I may not be able to comment on it for contractual reasons, but you can tell how I'm feeling about it by what I do or don't say. Am I talking about it? Promoting it? Sharing cool things? I am probably happy. Am I completely ignoring it and never mentioning it on social media? Yeah . . . that's probably not a good sign. For instance, check out my website, rickriordan.com. Do you see any indication there that the Percy Jackson movies ever existed? No. No, you do not."[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) He also shared in the same email "The script as a whole is terrible. I don't simply mean that it deviates from the book, though certainly it does that to point of being almost unrecognizable as the same story. Fans of the books will be angry and disappointed."[[34]](#cite_note-34)

#### Chris Columbus' response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Chris Columbus' response")]

In a 2025 interview with *GQ*, Chris Columbus revealed that after the movie came out, he realized that he "was alienating a good part of the audience who loved these books." He also added that "[he] put [him]self in a situation for the first time, probably only time in my career, where I was approaching a project not as a full-fledged adoring fan. When I got into *Harry Potter*, I was a fanboy first and foremost. When I got into *Percy Jackson*, I like the books, they're fine, but I think I can make them better. Huge mistake. I felt badly about that because I changed certain elements of the books because I thought it would be more interesting to me as a filmmaker."[[35]](#cite_note-35)

## Video game

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Video game")]

A video game based on the film developed by [Griptonite Games](/wiki/Griptonite_Games "Griptonite Games") and published by [Activision](/wiki/Activision "Activision") was released exclusively for [Nintendo DS](/wiki/Nintendo_DS "Nintendo DS") on February 11, 2010.[[41]](#cite_note-41) GameZone's Michael Splechta gave it a 6/10, saying "*Percy Jackson* might not make a splash when it comes to movie tie-in games, but fans of turn-based combat might find some redeeming qualities in this otherwise bare-bones game."[[42]](#cite_note-gamezone-42) On [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), the game has a score of 56 out of 100 based on 6 reviews, indicating "mixed or average reviews".[[43]](#cite_note-43)

## Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Home media")]

The film was released on June 29, 2010, on DVD[[6]](#cite_note-DVD-6) and Blu-ray.[[7]](#cite_note-Bluray-7) The movie itself charted at the top of the charts (DVD sales) with $13,985,047 in revenue in its first week.[[44]](#cite_note-44) As of October 2011, the movie had sold 2,087,368 DVDs with over $37 million in sales.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

## Sequel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Percy_Jackson_%26_the_Olympians:_The_Lightning_Thief&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Sequel")]

Main article: [Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters](/wiki/Percy_Jackson:_Sea_of_Monsters "Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters")

In October 2011, [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox") announced a sequel based on the second book, *[The Sea of Monsters](/wiki/The_Sea_of_Monsters "The Sea of Monsters")*.[[46]](#cite_note-46) The film was released on August 7, 2013.[[47]](#cite_note-47)
